name: Arizona Dietitian Registration
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Arizona - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: No Dedicated State Licensing Board
provider.parentOrganization: Arizona does not mandate state dietitian licensure. The national CDR credential is the recognized standard.

additionalType: Registered Dietitian (RD/RDN) — No State License Required
renewalCycle: No state renewal. CDR registration renews every 5 years with 75 CPE hours.

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Card
requiredDocuments.description: Your active CDR card serves as your primary credential in Arizona.

requiredDocuments.name: ACEND Verification Statement
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of completion of your accredited degree program and supervised practice.

mainEntity.name: Does Arizona require a state dietitian license?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Arizona does not mandate state-specific licensure for dietitians. Your national CDR registration as a Registered Dietitian (RD) or Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) is the recognized credential.

mainEntity.name: What does it cost to practice as a dietitian in Arizona?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no state application or renewal fee. The CDR national exam costs $200, and the annual CDR maintenance fee is $80 (increasing to $90 in 2027).

mainEntity.name: Is there pending legislation to require state licensure in Arizona?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of 2026, Arizona does not have an active, enacted state licensure law for dietitians. Legislative efforts (such as H.B. 2820 from 2021) have been introduced in the past.

mainEntity.name: Does Arizona have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: Arizona is currently a CDR State. There is no separate state endorsement transfer pathway right now; maintain an active CDR credential to practice.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Arizona?

acceptedAnswer.text: This is currently a CDR State, so there is no state transfer timeline. You can usually start once your CDR credential and employer onboarding are complete.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Arizona while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no active state endorsement application to wait on right now. Start dates are typically driven by employer credentialing and onboarding requirements.
